INT. BOARDROOM - DAY

A long mahogany table dominates the room. Six chairs on each side. At the head, DIRECTOR COLE, 50s, silver-haired, taps a folder against the table.

Around the table sit: VICE PRESIDENT SHARMA, 40s, sharp eyes; ANALYST PARK, 20s, nervous, fidgeting with a pen; CONSULTANT WRIGHT, 30s, confident, leaning back; and ASSISTANT TORRES, 30s, taking notes.

DIRECTOR COLE
Let's get started. Sharma, the numbers.

VICE PRESIDENT SHARMA
We're down twelve percent from last quarter. The European market tanked. Asia held steady. Domestic is flat.

DIRECTOR COLE
Flat is the new down. Park?

ANALYST PARK
(stumbling)
I — yes. The data shows consumer confidence dropped across all demographics. Especially the eighteen to thirty-four bracket.

CONSULTANT WRIGHT
Which is exactly what I warned you about in March.

DIRECTOR COLE
Nobody asked for an autopsy, Wright. What's the fix?

CONSULTANT WRIGHT sits up. Opens a sleek laptop. Projects a graph onto the wall screen.

CONSULTANT WRIGHT
We pivot. Rebrand the mid-tier line as premium. Cut the budget on legacy products. Reinvest in digital.

VICE PRESIDENT SHARMA
That's a three-quarter turnaround minimum. We don't have three quarters.

CONSULTANT WRIGHT
Then we accelerate. Hire a new creative team. Bring in fresh blood.

ASSISTANT TORRES
(without looking up)
Budget for that is already allocated. Line item fourteen.

Everyone looks at TORRES.

ASSISTANT TORRES
I read ahead.

DIRECTOR COLE
Almost smiled there, Torres. Don't scare the analysts.

ANALYST PARK
nervous laugh
I'm not scared. Much.

VICE PRESIDENT SHARMA
The real question is whether the board will approve the rebrand. Last time we proposed something like this, they sat on it for six months.

DIRECTOR COLE
Then we don't give them six months. We give them a vote on Friday.

CONSULTANT WRIGHT
That's aggressive.

DIRECTOR COLE
Aggressive is watching this company bleed out while we deliberate. Sharma, prepare the revised projections. Park, I want the consumer data broken down by region. Wright, draft the rebrand proposal. Torres —

ASSISTANT TORRES
Already on it.

DIRECTOR COLE
Good. Meeting adjourned.

They all rise. SHARMA lingers.

VICE PRESIDENT SHARMA
Cole. A word?

DIRECTOR COLE
After you clear the room.

TORRES herds the others out. The door closes. SHARMA and COLE face each other across the table.

VICE PRESIDENT SHARMA
You know this won't work.

DIRECTOR COLE
It has to work.

VICE PRESIDENT SHARMA
The board will block it. They always do.

DIRECTOR COLE
Not this time. I have the votes.

VICE PRESIDENT SHARMA
(surprised)
Since when?

DIRECTOR COLE
Since I made some calls last night.

They stare at each other. The tension is palpable.

FADE OUT.
